title: Amit Shah to review Manipur security situation tomorrow; CM likely to visit Churachandpur, a first since 2023 violence
content: Mr. Shah will hold the meeting with Manipur government’s Security Adviser Kuldiep Singh and State Director General of Police Mukesh Singh, Director General of Military Operations (DGMO) and D-Gs of Assam Rifles, Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) and Border Security Force (BSF); CM Yumnam Khemchand Singh is not part of Saturday’s meeting

title: SIT likely to re-audit Ram Temple Trust accounts for last five years
content: Repeat audit likely to scrutinise trust’s accounts over a five year period, covering construction-related expenditure as well as donations; SIT members speak with people at Ram Temple on July 3

title: Opposition’s letter to CJI: BJP alleges ‘dangerous conspiracy’ to malign Indian democracy
content: BJP MP Sudhanshu Trivedi describes letter written by 23 Opposition parties to Chief Justice of India as “attack on democracy”

title: Madras High Court grants time to the authorities to submit the report on joint survey of Thirupparankundram hill
content: The Tamil Nadu government sought time, arguing that certain areas needed to be resurveyed. The court said any expansion carried out after the 1931 Privy Council judgment should be mentioned in the report to be filed by August 14

title: IT Ministry to summon Meta officials over report on child sexual abuse advertisements on Instagram
content: The BBC had reported that it was shown over two dozen child sexual abuse material-related advertisments on Instagram, with links to Telegram channels

title: Feminist organisations demand reforms in third phase of SIR
content: The key demands of All-India Feminist Alliance include expanding eligibility criteria, shifting away from patriarchal lineage and household proofs to recognise alternative documentation, create safe linkage methods for those estranged from families

title: Kuppam not just a constituency but an emotion, says A.P. Chief Minister Naidu
content: The Chief Minister unveils ‘Swarna Kuppam Vision-2029,’ asserts that the government will pursue welfare, development, and good governance simultaneously while ensuring employment for every youth, higher incomes for farmers, and entrepreneurship opportunities for every family

title: Ram temple row: Congress wants PM to break silence, questions SIT probe
content: Party questions the probe by the SIT, demands immediate dissolution of Ram Janmabhoomi Trust and a Supreme Court-monitored investigation into its functioning and financial transactions

title: Industry-integrated training vital for AI-driven job market: APSCHE chief
content: Madhu Murthy says statewide initiative will equip 50,000 students with AI skills; 20 receive offer letters under Cohort 2025-26

title: Channi holds show of strength, Randhawa-Shah meet sets of speculation amid Punjab Congress reshuffle
content: Amid the growing clamour over the Punjab Congress revamp, Sukhjinder Singh Randhawa’s meeting with Home Minister Amit Shah adds to the buzz, though the Congress MP says it was on law and order

title: Interdisciplinary research key to scientific innovation: OU Professor
content: Kavi Kishor urges collaboration across disciplines to develop practical technologies and secure funding for multidisciplinary research

title: State to revive defunct lift irrigation schemes, says Rama Naidu
content: Inaugurating restoration works on the Vedadri-Kanchala Lift Irrigation Scheme, the Minister says ₹4,500 crore will be spent on repairing and maintaining lift irrigation schemes across the State

title: Watch: Anitha Radhakrishnan’s arrest triggers DMK-TVK standoff
content: Former Tamil Nadu Fisheries Minister and Tiruchendur DMK MLA Anitha R. Radhakrishnan has been arrested over his alleged remarks against Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay, triggering a sharp political confrontation between the ruling TVK and the Opposition DMK.

title: Kombukuthy residents alarmed as elephants breach mitigation measures
content: Despite a ₹7.20 crore investment by the Forest department in wildlife mitigation measures like trenches and electric fencing, the elephant breached these defences, raising serious concerns about their effectiveness.

title: JSW plant in Kadapa finalised during YSRCP term, claims Jagan
content: He congratulates JSW Steel MD Sajjan Jindal on restarting the plant in Rayalaseema, calls it a significant milestone in the industrial development of the region

title: Delhi CM launches EV subsidy portal
content: The portal will also serve as a single platform for all key information related to the Delhi Electric Vehicle Policy 2026

title: Jupally waits for BRS leaders at Press Club after throwing a challenge on Telangana debts
content: Excise Minister said Telangana’s total outstanding liabilities increased from ₹90,161 crore at the time of State formation in June 2014 to ₹8,21,651 crore by December 1, 2023, when the BRS left office

title: Deceptive stone-laying events of erstwhile regimes delayed steel plant project in Kadapa, says Naidu
content: After I laid the stone for the project in 2018, my successor Jagan Mohan Reddy laid foundation  twice in 2019 and 2023 for the same project, with no visible outcome, recalls the Chief Minister

title: Watch: INDIA bloc writes to CJI over SIR, EC misconduct | Above the Fold | 03.07.2026
content: The episode looks at the INDIA bloc’s joint memorandum to the Chief Justice of India; the Allahabad High Court’s stance on the Places of Worship Act; Russia-Ukraine war; former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei state funeral, Uddhav Thackeray’s “Ram Raksha” protest, latest FIFA updates, and more.

title: U.S. technology company’s move to shut down centres in Kerala taking down around 800 jobs halted
content: Employees in Kochi and Kozhikode offices of the firm CorroHealth Infotech Private Limited were terminated reportedly without prior notice

title: Forest Department enforces ban on entry to forests and water falls
content: This follows the death of Aditya Satpati, 20, from Belagavi, who drowned while bathing at the Barajan waterfall, located in the core protected zone of the Mhadei Wildlife Sanctuary in Goa, last week

title: Mavigun is a better, cost-effective option than Amaravati, says Sajjala Ramakrishna Reddy
content: Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu is not in a position to explain how Amaravati is a self-financing project and why he borrowed over ₹47,000 crore, remarks YSRCP leader

title: Tech-driven value addition need of the hour for Indian agriculture: Shobha Karandlaje
content: ‘Agriculture is rapidly transforming into a technology-driven business ecosystem where innovation, deep-tech, sustainability and value-chain development are becoming central to economic growth’

title: CET opens global research pathways for students
content: The project brings together international experts from reputed institutions to mentor students and faculty members in emerging research areas

title: Tribal Ashram School warden carries sick girl on shoulders for six km to ensure timely medical care
content: Hema had to undertake the arduous task in the absence of medical facility in the school and road and transport facility to the hospital located in the mandal headquarters of Gummalakshmipuram

title: Centre sets up panel to study problems of Andhra’s Totapuri mango farmers
content: During their interactions, farmers told Union Minister for Agriculture Shivraj Singh Chouhan that sustained fall in prices adversely affected their income and created financial stress among Totapuri mango-producing agriculturists

title: Jarkiholi tells officials to be prepared for possible water scarcity
content: The Minister directs the officials in the Minor Irrigation and Irrigation Department to immediately take steps to fill up tanks in Haveri district

title: No engine damage: Govt issues 10-point clarification on E20 ethanol blending programme
content: Government has strongly refuted social media misinformation regarding India's E20 ethanol blending program.  Extensive testing confirms no significant adverse effects on vehicles, engines, or fuel safety.  Claims about excessive water use and environmental risks are also debunked, highlighting the program's benefits in foreign exchange savings and farmer payments.  The initiative is presented as scientifically backed and globally adopted.

title: 'Why are the police here?' Wife's chilling remark after killing husband of nearly 30 years
content: A woman has been sentenced to life imprisonment for murdering her husband of nearly 30 years, with a minimum of 12 years before parole eligibility. The court rejected her claim that the fatal stabbing was an accident, finding she deliberately attacked him during an altercation. Body-camera footage released by police shows her appearing confused and questioning why officers were present, stating, "You don't think I've murdered him, do you?"

title: 'It's not straightforward': India coach's clear message on Sooryavanshi debut
content: India's bowling coach Morne Morkel affirmed continued backing for openers Sanju Samson and Abhishek Sharma, despite calls for 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's debut. Morkel emphasised faith in proven performers and avoiding disrupting batting order. He praised Sooryavanshi's smooth integration and potential readiness for future opportunities, while also commending pacers Prince Yadav and Harshit Rana for their impressive performances.

title: Oil slips to pre-Iran war levels: Is a petrol, diesel price cut coming?
content: Petrol and diesel prices in India have risen by around Rs 7.5 per litre since May 15. The first hike in the retail rates came over two and a half months after the US-Iran war began. Private retailer Nayara Energy has already cut rates of petrol and diesel.

title: After years of tracking packages, Amazon planning to use the same playbook for employees
content: Amazon is reportedly deploying wearable devices to precisely track warehouse employees in support roles, previously monitored manually. This new 'Right Station Link' program aims to automate data collection, eliminate human error, and optimize worker placement, potentially saving billions. The move follows Amazon's success in tracking frontline workers and addresses concerns about overstaffing in mobile positions.

title: India’s women-owned business map has a western tilt
content: Women's entrepreneurship is growing in India, but unevenly. Cities in Gujarat and Maharashtra lead in female-owned businesses, while northern cities lag. This disparity highlights the complex interplay of economic structure, local conditions, and societal norms. While government initiatives are noted, deeper empowerment hinges on grassroots changes within homes and local markets, suggesting a patchwork of progress rather than a uniform national trend.

title: Self-immolation outside UN draws fresh attention to Tibet
content: A Tibetan activist's self-immolation outside the UN headquarters in New York has reignited global attention on Tibet's human rights crisis. Lobga Rangzen's desperate act, protesting Chinese rule and new assimilation laws, highlights the fading international focus on the issue. This tragic event, occurring after over 150 similar protests in Tibet, challenges governments to confront the unresolved situation, forcing a difficult conversation amidst strained US-China relations.

title: Days after Meta Layoffs, Zuckerberg says companies should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, while scrapping thousands of open roles.  The company is heavily investing in AI infrastructure, aiming for efficiency amidst a competitive landscape.  This stance contrasts with other industry leaders' more cautious outlooks on AI's impact on employment.

title: 'Fastest man alive' Usain Bolt turns up for Djokovic's Wimbledon clash - Watch
content: Videos and pictures of Bolt watching the match quickly spread across social media. Even years after retiring from athletics, the eight-time Olympic gold medallist continues to draw attention wherever he goes.

title: Inside Patrick and Brittany Mahomes’ $8M mansion and its dreamy living room
content: Patrick and Brittany Mahomes have unveiled their extraordinary $8 million dream home in Belton, Missouri, nestled on an eight-acre property. Completed in 2023, this magnificent mansion boasts recreation areas including a 50-yard football field, a par-3 golf hole, and a basketball court, all harmonized within its warm minimalist aesthetic. The neutral palette throughout creates a serene environment tailored for their family of five and their beloved pets.

title: Amazon spider looked so much like a fungus, scientists first mistook it
content: In a groundbreaking discovery in Ecuador's Amazon, scientists have identified a unique spider species, Taczanowskia waska, which artfully impersonates a fungus that preys on spiders. This exceptional adaptation combines pale hues, fungus-like appendages, and a still demeanor that interlaces it seamlessly within its environment, deceiving even seasoned researchers. Highlighted through citizen science, this discovery underscores the complex survival tactics in nature and the enigmatic diversity of rainforests.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.

title: Badrinath-Kedarnath Temple Committee orders inquiry after claims of donation theft
content: Allegations of misappropriated donations at Badrinath Dham have prompted an inquiry by the Shri Badrinath-Kedarnath Temple Committee. Chairman Hemant Dwivedi confirmed an investigation is underway, with explanations sought from staff. Strict action will follow if guilt is established. This follows similar scrutiny at the Ayodhya temple, where financial irregularities are being probed.

title: How a cricket technology saved Ronaldo's FIFA World Cup dream - Explained
content: Portugal secured victory through Ronaldo's penalty and a stoppage-time header from Goncalo Ramos. But the biggest talking point came in the closing minutes when Croatia had a late equaliser ruled out after a VAR review.

title: Jets' $68M star linked to Sabres, Hurricanes in blockbuster trade buzz
content: The Winnipeg Jets signing of Stuart Skinner has set off rumors on the future of the franchise’s goaltender, Connor Hellebuyck. There are no trade talks confirmed, but league observers believe the move gives Winnipeg more room to maneuver should the organization decide to make significant roster changes this NHL offseason.

title: Usain Bolt, Kasi Bennett's 2026 net worth: Earnings, endorsements and lifestyle
content: Nine years post-retirement, Usain Bolt's global icon status fuels a $90 million fortune, bolstered by enduring endorsements and business ventures. His partner, Kasi Bennett, independently contributes between $1-2 million through her entrepreneurial and philanthropic work. Together, this Jamaican power couple boasts a combined net worth nearing $92 million, showcasing a successful blend of family, luxury, and expanding business interests.

title: Bruins veteran reportedly blocked Darnell Nurse trade using no-trade clause
content: Darnell Nurse was involved in trade talks that extended past the eventual deal to the San Jose Sharks, according to reports. A new report says the Edmonton Oilers were talking trade with the Boston Bruins until a player's contract protection got in the way. The development adds a new chapter to one of the most talked-about trade stories in the NHL this offseason.

title: Jason Kelce and Taylor Swift’s fiance Travis Kelce interview Prince William
content: In a surprising move, Prince William appeared on the New Heights podcast. This diverts the attention from the highly anticipated Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding. The interview features a grand introduction for the prince. It probably promises to reveal details and potentially shift focus from wedding. Did Prince William plat cupid? Because NFL sportscaster Erin Andrews is credited as a cupid in the couple’s romance. To top it, she was present at their wedding rehearsal dinner.

title: PM Modi shares glimpse of new Jodhpur airport ahead of inauguration - see pics
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the new Jodhpur airport terminal, a significant boost for tourism and connectivity, blending modern design with Rajasthani heritage. He also launched the Modified UDAN scheme from Jodhpur, aiming to enhance regional air connectivity nationwide by developing 100 aerodromes and supporting airline operations. This infrastructure push is set to invigorate commerce and travel in the region.

title: TMC vs TMC: Ritabrata faction takes control of party HQ as rebellion intensifies
content: A significant power struggle has erupted within the Trinamool Congress as a rebel faction, led by Ritabrata Banerjee, seized control of the party's Kolkata headquarters. This move follows their appeal to the Election Commission to claim the party's name and symbol. The faction asserts its legitimacy amidst ongoing disputes with the Mamata Banerjee-led camp, escalating the internal conflict over organisational leadership.

title: 5 sun-loving houseplants that are perfect for hot summer days
content: City dwellers often struggle with keeping houseplants alive during scorching summers. Fortunately, several resilient plants thrive in heat and direct sunlight, requiring minimal fuss. Discover five easy-care sun-lovers like the tough Snake Plant, the multi-purpose Aloe Vera, the vibrant Bougainvillea, the energetic Portulaca, and the sophisticated Bay Laurel. These green companions can transform urban spaces into lush sanctuaries without demanding constant attention.

title: Crude cools down: What falling oil prices mean for your wallet and markets — explained in 10 charts
content: Global crude prices have cooled, bringing relief to India's economy. While consumers may not see immediate fuel price drops due to inventory costs, the nation benefits from a reduced import bill, easing inflation, and improved government finances. Sectors like aviation are already seeing positive impacts, and economic growth forecasts are being upgraded, signaling a brighter outlook.

title: Ketan Agarwal case: Friend who 'knew of alleged plot' claims he urged Chetan, Siya not to kill
content: A friend of one of the accused in the Pune realtor's murder case has been taken in for questioning. Police believe he was privy to the alleged plot to kill Ketan Agarwal and had even warned the accused against it. The youth, who was reportedly in contact with the accused since late May, was allegedly urged to join them on the day of the incident at Lohagad Fort but declined.

title: 'We are talking too big about Vaibhav Sooryavanshi': Kapil Dev makes big claim
content: Excitement is building for 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's potential India debut against England. Despite a sensational IPL, he awaits his international bow. Legendary Kapil Dev, while acknowledging his immense talent akin to Tendulkar and Kohli, urges caution against excessive hype, emphasising the need for him to prove himself in longer formats.  The debate intensifies as India seeks a turnaround in the T20 series.

title: India set to contest US tariff move, calls 'forced labour' findings legally flawed
content: India is set to contest proposed US tariffs on its exports next week, arguing that the US Trade Representative's findings on alleged forced labour are legally flawed. Indian officials and industry bodies will present counter-arguments, emphasizing the country's robust legal framework and compliance systems. They contend the tariffs would harm both Indian exporters and American businesses and consumers.

title: 'Highest highs to lowest lows': Ben Stokes reflects on 'mad 15 years'
content: Stokes announced his retirement during the third Test against New Zealand at Trent Bridge. Moments after issuing an official statement, he claimed his final wicket for England.

title: 'Goli maar denge': BJP leader, aide booked for molesting, threatening doctor at MP hospital
content: A BJP leader and an associate face charges for allegedly molesting a female postgraduate doctor, abusing hospital staff, and threatening to shoot them at Sagar's Bundelkhand Medical College. The incident, which occurred during a patient admission attempt, triggered a massive protest by medical and nursing staff demanding immediate arrest and better security. Services resumed after assurances, but healthcare workers warned of further action if the accused are not apprehended swiftly.

title: 'Pak must stop backing terror': India on why Indus Waters Treaty still 'in abeyance'
content: India's stance on the Indus Waters Treaty remains firm, with the agreement suspended due to Pakistan's continued support for cross-border terrorism. The MEA reiterated this position, emphasizing Pakistan's need to cease its sponsorship of terror. Meanwhile, India has communicated its views on the proposed Teesta River project to Bangladesh, assuring cooperation based on mutual understanding and consultations.

title: India sets $1 trillion export goal for FY26 as merchandise, services trade rise
content: India aims for a monumental $1 trillion in exports this fiscal year, a goal enthusiastically embraced by industry and policymakers. Union Minister Piyush Goyal confirmed the ambitious target, highlighting strong growth in both merchandise and services sectors. Recent free trade agreements and a seven-point action plan are set to bolster this drive, with a significant push in defence exports also noted.

title: Pak Deputy PM's relative among 4 held over assault on 2 foreign women
content: Pakistani authorities have apprehended four individuals, including a relative of Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar, in connection with the alleged gang rape of two foreign women in Lahore. The victims, from the Netherlands and Venezuela, reportedly met the main accused, Muhammad Raza Dar, during a cryptocurrency venture in Singapore. They were allegedly abducted and assaulted after arriving in Pakistan for business purposes. Investigations are ongoing.

title: Aamir Khan, Gauri Spratt to marry under Special Marriage Act; 150 guests expected
content: Bollywood star Aamir Khan is set to marry his girlfriend Gauri Spratt on July 5 in a private ceremony. The actor confirmed the intimate wedding, which will be attended by close family and friends. The couple has been actively involved in planning the event, which includes a marriage registration followed by a lunch featuring their favorite dishes. Khan expressed his desire for everyone's blessings for this special occasion.

title: 'They're best by a mile': Pak coach calls out India obsession in judging his team
content: Pakistan coach Mike Hesson argues his team's progress shouldn't be solely judged by results against India, highlighting the current Indian side's dominance over every team. He pointed out Pakistan's strong performance in other matches, emphasizing their significant improvement in win percentages over the past year, moving from a low standing to winning a majority of their games.

title: PM Modi to begin three-nation tour to Indonesia, New Zealand and Australia from July 6
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is set for a significant three-nation tour of Indonesia, Australia, and New Zealand from July 6-11. This visit aims to bolster India's ties with these nations. Notably, his trip to New Zealand marks the first by an Indian Prime Minister in nearly four decades, highlighting the growing importance of the recently signed Free Trade Agreement between the two countries.

title: Chandrayaan-3 findings link landing site to first-known lunar meteorite
content: Chandrayaan-3's Pragyan rover has unveiled lunar soil that closely resembles the first confirmed Moon meteorite, ALHA 81005. This groundbreaking finding links the 'Shiv Shakti Station' with a meteorite discovered in Antarctica, suggesting a shared origin from a magnesium-rich lunar crust. Researchers propose that this material was raised to the surface during the ancient impact that created the South Pole-Aitken basin, providing valuable insights into the Moon's formative geological processes.

title: NHL Trade Rumor: Red Wings, Hurricanes eye $59M Winnipeg Jets star
content: The Winnipeg Jets’ star player Connor Hellebuyck has been dealing with rumors regarding his trade from the team after they signed Stuart Skinner, ahead of the new season. Amid all the mess, it seems the Detroit Red Wings and the Carolina Hurricanes have emerged as potential landing spots for the star player.

title: Big boost to armed forces, defence ministry clears equipment worth Rs 50,000 crore
content: The Defence Ministry has greenlit significant acquisitions exceeding Rs 52,000 crore, bolstering national security. Key approvals include advanced anti-drone systems, indigenous anti-tank missiles, and enhanced air defence capabilities for both Army and Air Force. Naval and Air Force modernization also sees boosts with new mine systems, unmanned aerial vehicles, and high-altitude surveillance platforms, ensuring readiness against evolving threats.

title: WhatsApp username row: Meta team meets govt officials after notice, final reply due in 3 days
content: Meta officials met with Indian IT ministry representatives following a government notice regarding WhatsApp's new username feature. New Delhi expressed concerns that the feature could facilitate cybercrime, including impersonation and fraud. WhatsApp has stated the feature is not yet live and will include safeguards like optional usage and reserved prominent usernames to prevent misuse. The government awaits a final response before allowing the rollout.

title: Fear of US strikes? Mojtaba Khamenei 'absent' from his father's funeral service
content: Iran mourns its Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, killed in recent Middle East conflict. His funeral, expected to draw millions and foreign dignitaries, is under heightened security due to potential attacks. Notably, his son and successor, Mojtaba Khamenei, was absent from initial ceremonies. Burial plans have been delayed amid security concerns, with events scheduled across Iran and Iraq.

title: England's Playing XI vs India in 2nd T20I: Archer IN; ENG make two big changes
content: England has brought back Jofra Archer and handed a T20I debut to Josh Tongue for the second T20I against India in Manchester. The opening match was abandoned due to rain after India posted a strong 189/7, with Abhishek Sharma and Shreyas Iyer starring with the bat. Both teams now aim to take a crucial lead in the series.

title: Govt pulls plug on Chinese apps used to stop e-rickshaws
content: In a significant move, authorities have instructed Google and Apple to take down three Chinese battery management applications—BAT-BMS, Lossigy, and Epoch-i-ion—from their platforms. The apps have allegedly been exploited to remotely shut down battery-operated vehicles like e-rickshaws, endangering the livelihoods of drivers. This highlights a concerning trend, shifting the focus from mere data security to tangible interference with electric vehicles operating on Indian streets, prompting further investigative scrutiny.

title: Provident Fund Over Rs 1,800 Optional: Why It's Hard to Ignore 8.25% Returns
content: Provident Funds have been one of the most reliable retirement plans for Indians. Given its low-risk and tax-free returns, the instrument is widely famous across the working class.

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Many Parts Of Gujarat Flooded After Rain, Schools Declare Holiday Near Mumbai
content: Strong westerly winds likely to prevail over the southern peninsular India and central India till July 8, the India Meteorological Department said.

title: Agra Woman Kills Husband, Buries Body In Bathroom. Tells People He's Missing
content: The police along with some workers broke a layer of plaster on the bathroom floor to recover the body

title: 87 Staff, Transfers, No Building: Inside Indore's 6-Year-Old 'Ghost Hospital'
content: Khajrana Civil Hospital in Indore was approved in 2020 but has neither land nor a building. However, 87 government posts have been sanctioned.

title: Trinamool vs Trinamool As Rebel Faction 'Takes Control' Of Party Office
content: The rebels also put up a poster on the front gate identifying senior MLA Arup Roy as chairman of the All India Trinamool Congress. Photographs and cutouts of Mamata Banerjee in the building were left in place.

title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei Funeral News Updates: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's Funeral Begins In Iran
content: Ali Khamenei's Last Rites News Live: Preparations for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's public funeral are taking place in Tehran, following which his remains will be taken to the Iraqi holy cities of Najaf and Karbala.

title: ISRO Bomb Threat Sent By Mentally Challenged Man, Targeted NIA, Air India Too
content: A 36-year-old man accused of sending hoax bomb threat emails to several of India's most sensitive institutions, including ISRO, the National Investigation Agency (NIA) and an Air India flight, has been arrested from Uttar Pradesh's Ghaziabad, Delhi Police said.

title: Kamikaze Drones, Anti-Tank Weapons In India's Rs 52,000-Crore Defence Cart
content: The acceptance of necessity (AoN) include the approval of indigenous as well as advanced defence equipment for the armed forces, focusing primarily on air defence, drone-killing equipment, surveillance and unmanned systems

title: Europe's Deadly Heatwave: Why The World Is Warming At Different Speeds
content: Global temperatures accelerated through June, with the daily average surface air temperature reaching 16.80 degrees Celsius on June 30, 2026, according to Copernicus' ERA5 dataset.

title: Global Network Used Chat Groups To Drug, Rape Women In 9 Countries, Busted
content: The investigation, involving authorities from seven countries, has revealed how online chat groups were being used to exchange advice on drugging victims, avoiding detection and circulating videos and images of the abuse.

title: EU Sanctions 6 People Involved In Death Of Putin Critic Alexei Navalny
content: The EU said the people were involved with the development of chemical weapons, particularly epibatidine, that were found in samples taken from the activist's body after his death.

title: Activists Allowed To Sue Sweden Over Failure To Tackle Climate Change
content: Activist groups around the world are increasingly looking to the courts to force governments and corporations to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and better protect the environment.

title: Ocean Temperatures In June Surge Past Extreme 2023-24 El Nino Records
content: Right now, the average sea surface temperature is just under 21 degrees Celsius across the world's tropical and temperate oceans.

title: Meta Officials Hold Discussions With Centre Over Username Feature: Sources
content: The government fears that the username feature will be misused by cyberthugs for impersonations, identity spoofing, phishing, and other online scams.

title: "Undermining Democracy": BJP Slams Opposition Over Letter To Chief Justice
content: "The BJP strongly condemns the letter written to the Chief Justice of India, and the failed attempt to undermine democracy, by a few political parties..." BJP MP Sudhanshu Trivedi said

title: She Was Sacked Day After Exposing Bengaluru Daycare Horror: Whistleblower's Son
content: Karnataka Home Minister Priyank Kharge has asserted that the government has "zero tolerance" for crimes involving children and said daycare centres must strictly adhere to Standard Operating Procedures

title: Siya Goyal, Lover Chetan Sent To 14-day Judicial Custody In Pune Murder Case
content: A Pune court on Friday sent Siya Goyal and her alleged lover Chetan Chaudhary, the accused in the purported murder of Ketan Agarwal, to 14 days of judicial custody.

title: Domestic Help Steals Rs 10 Lakh Jewellery, Caught After Flaunting It On Reel
content: Nitisha Vats, who lives in Sikka Kimaya Greens in Dehradun's IT Park, reported to police that the theft took place about a year ago when she was residing in Doon Republic Apartments in Bhagirathi Puram in Jakhan.

title: BJP Youth Leader Molests Doctor, Threatens To Shoot Her, Case Filed
content: The accused, Anil Srivastava, a member of the BJP State Yuva Morcha executive committee, and his associate have been booked by Sagar police under multiple provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS).

title: Centre Orders Removal Of 7 Apps Used To Stop E-Rickshaws In Delhi
content: MeitY Secretary S Krishnan said the government will take up this issue with the app stores to ensure potentially harmful applications don't come up in the public domain.
